trivia_bullets.text: Prosperous Pirates is a reprint (Scryfall: reprint: true), originating in Ixalan (XLN, September 2017), MTG's pirate-and-dinosaur plane. Ixalan's pirates — the Brazen Coalition — are treasure-obsessed raiders sailing the Stormwreck Sea in search of the golden city of Orazca. The flavor text references the floating city of High and Dry, a pirate settlement built on hoarded treasure, suggesting a location not formally named in Ixalan's story.

Flavor text: "The floating city of High and Dry hasn't yet sunk under the weight of gold, but it's not for want of trying." The line is narrated with dry humor — 'High and Dry' evokes both a nautical idiom (stranded, helpless) and literal elevation (the city floats above water). The joke is that the pirates are sinking their own city with accumulated wealth.

Prosperous Pirates creates two Treasure tokens on ETB — five mana for a 3/4 plus two any-color mana rocks. At EDHREC rank 15287 it sees consistent Commander play in Treasure-matters and pirate tribal decks. The Treasure token type was introduced in Ixalan and has since become one of the most-supported token types in Commander, making this card's original Ixalan printing a foundational piece of that design lineage.

The Scryfall all_parts field links Prosperous Pirates to the Treasure token card, confirming it as a designed component of the Treasure token economy. CMR included multiple pirate cards — including Azure Fleet Admiral (CMR 58) — as a creature-type cohort. Prosperous Pirates is the most mechanically significant of that pirate common cluster due to the Treasure token ramp payoff in Commander.
